lever.description: Reuse the existing eval_unary_f64_tensor_fast_path at the top of eval_unary_int_or_float's Tensor arm. Previously Square/Sign over F64 tensors ran a full per-element `match literal { ... }` over every Literal variant plus an out-dtype match; the fast path loops F64Bits directly applying float_op. Returns None (fall through) for non-F64 tensors.

baseline.note: fast-path guard temporarily removed; generic per-element variant match

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/square_1k_f64'

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output elements produced in tensor index order; identical mapping per position.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: from_f64(float_op(from_bits(b))) (generic) == F64Bits(float_op(from_bits(b)).to_bits()) (fast path); the same float_op closures are used.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: None introduced; square(-0.0)=+0.0, sign(-0.0)=-0.0, NaN->canonical NaN, +-inf preserved.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Only F64 tensors take the fast path; other dtypes/malformed literals use the generic per-element variant match.
